description_long: This object is a pair of metal scissors with a robust, utilitarian design, likely intended for heavy-duty or industrial use. The handles are circular and symmetrical, formed from the same material as the blades, giving the scissors a seamless, forged appearance. The blades are long, straight, and taper to a sharp, pointed tip, suitable for precise cutting tasks. The pivot joint is solid and appears to be a fixed rivet, providing a sturdy connection between the two blades. The entire surface has a dark, matte finish, suggesting it is made from blackened or oxidized steel. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, emphasizing function over aesthetics.
description: A heavy-duty pair of black metal scissors with circular handles and long, pointed blades, featuring a matte finish and a solid rivet joint.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical circular handles and the pointed blade tip.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the blades and the thickness of the handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the alignment of the handles and the straightness of the blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the blade length and handle profile.
attribute_handle_type: Symmetrical, circular metal handles, not padded or coated, ambidextrous, standard grip.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Fixed rivet joint, visible, not adjustable.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Two equal-sized, round loops with smooth edges, no inserts or texturing, accommodates standard finger sizes.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Pointed blade tips, designed for precision cutting.
